---
title: "LCSM-081: Does prior word reading predict taught-vocabulary change? (lagged reverse coupling)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LCSM-081 is the headline model of the **time-lagged reverse-coupling** suite
(#250): the founding RLI hypothesis — reading instruction feeding back into
language — as a direct test on the adopted wave-unrolled DAG. For each
wave-to-wave transition, does a child's prior-wave **word-reading** standing
predict their subsequent change in the block-1 **taught** vocabulary measures
(expressive `TE` and receptive `TR`), over and above the taught measures' own
carry-over, the intervention windows, and everything measured the lagged graph
names?

The design and the verified adjustment-set derivations are recorded in
`notes/202607141030-time-lagged-model-designs.md`. Two features follow directly
from those derivations:

1. **Arm × window change intercepts.** The waitlist crossover makes the
   randomised arm a confounder of every transition-2+ coupling (the waitlist
   arm's block-1 catch-up lands exactly in window 2), so each measure's change
   intercept is an arm × window cell rather than a pooled constant. The
   window-1 cell contrast is a randomised latent ITT contrast, reported below
   as a consistency check against the ITT suite. Sessions stay out of the
   change equations (the `IS` collider, ID-3).
2. **The adjuster block completes the measured backdoor set.** With the `TR`
   process plus time-invariant hearing (`hs`) and prior-wave phonological
   memory (`erbto`) and speech production (`deapp_c`), the conditioning set for
   each headline coupling equals the verified sufficient measured set — latent
   general ability aside, which no measured set can block.

## Model structure

For each measure $m \in \{TE, TR, W\}$, child $i$ and wave $t$, a latent logit
true-score $x_m[i,t]$ accumulates change scores (the LRP67 McArdle scaffold
with process noise). The change equations are:

$$
\begin{aligned}
\overline{\Delta}_{TE} &= a_{TE}[\text{arm}, w] + b_{TE}\,x_{TE} + g_{W \to TE}\,x_W
  + g_{TR \to TE}\,x_{TR} + d^{\text{age}}\,\text{age} + \text{(hs / rw / sp block)} \\
\overline{\Delta}_{TR} &= a_{TR}[\text{arm}, w] + b_{TR}\,x_{TR} + g_{W \to TR}\,x_W
  + d^{\text{age}}\,\text{age} + \text{(same block)} \\
\overline{\Delta}_{W} &= a_{W}[\text{arm}, w] + b_{W}\,x_W + d^{\text{age}}\,\text{age}
\end{aligned}
$$

with every prior-wave level $x[t-1]$. $g_{W \to TE}$ and $g_{W \to TR}$ are the
headline coefficients — prior **level** → subsequent **change**, pooled across
the three transitions, the same specification form as LRP67. The `W` equation
is deliberately bare: forward couplings into reading are LRP67's estimands, not
this model's. Observed counts enter a **masked** Beta-Binomial so a child
missing one score still contributes their other waves.

**Reading rule:** both headline couplings are **adjusted associations** —
latent general ability is unblockable, the independent initial statuses absorb
only part of the stable between-child differences, and the RI-CLPM that would
separate them is not estimable at this sample size. Only the window-1 arm
contrast is randomised. The pre-specified reading (design note): the RLI
hypothesis is supported in this cohort if both couplings are positive with
$P(g > 0) \ge 0.9$ and this model is not LOO-worse than the
no-reverse-coupling comparator LCSM-181.

{{< include _partials/_results_lcsm.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
